SLA Metric: Defect Density: ~22.19 bugs per KLOC (Expected: 2 bugs per KLOC)
Risk Description: Because of defect density at ~22.19 bugs per KLOC which is 11x higher than the expected baseline of 2 bugs per KLOC, there is a risk that quality gates will fail repeatedly, which could lead to extended QA cycles, delayed UAT acceptance, and loss of client confidence in delivery quality.
Tech Stack: Angular 18, .NET 8, SQL Server 2019, AWS, Azure, APIs
Potential Impact: Severe quality degradation requiring extensive rework, potential UAT rejection, schedule delays of 2-4 weeks, and reputational damage with Axos Bank stakeholders. May trigger contractual penalty clauses and impact future project phases.

Risk Description: Because of defect leakage at 4.8% which is approaching the critical threshold of 5%, there is a risk that production defects will increase post-deployment, which could lead to customer-facing issues, emergency hotfixes, and SLA breaches for Axos Bank's banking operations.
Potential Impact: Potential production incidents affecting end-users, increased support costs, emergency patch deployments, and possible SLA violations. Risk of impacting live banking operations for Axos Bank customers, leading to regulatory scrutiny and financial penalties.

Risk Description: Because of undefined performance testing strategy and unknown ability to meet stringent SLAs (UI page rendering ≤10 seconds, API response time ≤3 seconds) as indicated in the MBR, there is a risk that performance bottlenecks will be discovered late in the cycle, which could lead to major code refactoring, infrastructure capacity upgrades, and schedule delays.
Potential Impact: Significant code optimization required, potential infrastructure scaling costs, delays in production deployment by 3-6 weeks, and failure to meet contractual performance commitments. May require architectural changes and additional cloud resources, increasing project costs by 15-25%.

Risk Description: Because of undefined data migration strategy and unknown volume of work required to onboard 5 beta customers from legacy Zenith to NexLedgr as documented in both SOW and MBR, there is a risk that customer onboarding will be delayed or require manual data entry, which could lead to go-live delays, data integrity issues, and inability to meet Phase 2 production deployment commitments.
Tech Stack: C#, Python, SQL Server 2019, Legacy Zenith System
Potential Impact: Delayed customer onboarding, potential data loss or corruption, extended timeline for production readiness by 4-8 weeks, and risk of manual data entry errors. May impact contractual commitments for beta customer availability and delay revenue recognition.

Risk Description: Because of recurring network issues causing environment downtime in DEV/QA environments and delays in AWS Bedrock (Claude Sonnet) provisioning as documented in the MBR dependencies, there is a risk that development and testing activities will be blocked, which could lead to schedule slippage, reduced team productivity, and inability to complete milestone deliverables on time.
Tech Stack: AWS, Azure, Burpsuite, SonarQube, GoAnywhere, AAVA 2.0
Potential Impact: Development delays of 1-2 weeks per incident, blocked QA testing cycles, reduced team utilization, and cascading impact on downstream milestones. Email notification service downtime affects operational workflows. Estimated productivity loss of 15-20% during outage periods.

Risk Description: Because of API test automation at only 22% completion against the expected 50% baseline, and pending public/private API pipeline categorization as noted in MBR dependencies, there is a risk that regression testing will be inadequate and manual, which could lead to increased defect leakage, delayed release cycles, and inability to scale testing for 300+ APIs.
Potential Impact: Inadequate regression coverage leading to production defects, manual testing bottlenecks causing 2-3 week delays per release, and inability to maintain testing velocity as API count grows. Increased QA resource requirements and potential for critical API failures in production.

SLA Metric: Banking Integration: 3 modules pending (NACHA, Funds Transfer, Wire/ACH Confirmations)
Risk Description: Because of pending Axos Bank API availability for NACHA integration and incomplete HLDs for Funds Transfer, Wire, and ACH Confirmations as documented in the Module Readiness Summary, there is a risk that banking integration testing will be delayed, which could lead to incomplete end-to-end workflows, UAT blockers, and inability to demonstrate core banking functionality for Phase 2 go-live.
Tech Stack: Banking APIs, Entitlement, BAI2, NACHA, Axos Bank Integration
Potential Impact: Delayed banking integration causing UAT postponement by 3-4 weeks, incomplete core functionality for production deployment, and potential contractual breach with Axos Bank. Risk of manual workarounds or scope reduction impacting customer experience and business value delivery.

SLA Metric: Reporting Scope: 7 standard reports with SAVE AS (Milestone 1)
Risk Description: Because of ongoing POC reviews and scope prioritization discussions for Reporting Center with unresolved PowerBI capabilities and SAVE AS parameters as documented in the MBR Issues, there is a risk that reporting requirements will change mid-development, which could lead to rework, scope creep, and delays in Milestone 1 deliverables.
Tech Stack: PowerBI, Angular, Paginated Reports, SQL Server 2019
Potential Impact: Potential rework of 2-3 weeks if scope changes after development, delayed Milestone 1 completion, and misalignment with stakeholder expectations. May require re-estimation and change management process, impacting downstream milestones and overall Phase 2 timeline.

Risk Description: Because of project completion at only ~34% against the original plan baseline and pending approval on revised timelines as indicated in the MBR Status Summary, there is a risk that Phase 2 will not meet the mid-July/August 2026 target, which could lead to extended project duration, increased costs, and delayed business value realization for Axos Bank.
Tech Stack: Full Stack: Angular 18, .NET 8, AWS, Azure, SQL Server
Potential Impact: Schedule extension of 2-3 months beyond approved timeline, increased project costs due to extended resource engagement, and delayed ROI for Axos Bank. Potential impact on subsequent project phases and contractual commitments. Risk of stakeholder dissatisfaction and reduced confidence in delivery capability.

Financial Metric: Cost: AWS Bedrock Claude Sonnet 4.5 (CISO & Cost approvals secured)
Risk Description: Because of pending enablement of AWS Bedrock services in AXOS infrastructure as documented in the MBR Scope section, there is a risk that Billie Part 2 AI-powered invoice processing development will be blocked, which could lead to Milestone 1 delays, inability to demonstrate AI capabilities, and potential scope reduction for hybrid LLM+ML architecture.
Tech Stack: AWS Bedrock, Claude Sonnet 4.5, OCR, ML, LLM, AAVA 2.0
Potential Impact: Development blockage for AI-powered features causing 2-3 week delay in Milestone 1, reduced competitive differentiation, and inability to deliver approved hybrid architecture. May require fallback to traditional approaches, reducing business value and innovation impact for invoice processing automation.

Owner: Shared Services Team, Axos Enterprise Team
SLA Metric: Shared Services: USPS, RBAC, File Upload, Scheduler, Notifications
Risk Description: Because of pending shared services components (USPS address validation, RBAC, File Upload, Scheduler, Notifications) that are in refinement/grooming phase as documented in the MBR Key Highlights, there is a risk that dependent modules (Deposits, Receipts, Commissions) will be blocked, which could lead to cascading delays across Milestones 1-3 and incomplete end-to-end functionality.
Tech Stack: USPS API, RBAC, File Upload, Scheduler, Notifications, Enterprise Services
Potential Impact: Blocked development for multiple modules causing delays of 3-4 weeks across Milestones 1-3, incomplete integration testing, and reduced system functionality. Risk of manual workarounds or scope reduction impacting user experience and operational efficiency. May require expedited development with increased resource allocation.

Risk Description: Because of dependency on third-party provider Rollfi for payroll functionality with 59 APIs and 19 UI/UX screens as specified in the SOW Phase 2 Amendment, and potential for provider change requiring change management process, there is a risk that integration delays or provider limitations will occur, which could lead to functional parity gaps with legacy Zenith system and delayed payroll module delivery.
Tech Stack: Rollfi APIs, Payroll Integration, UI/UX, .NET 8, Angular 18
Potential Impact: Delayed payroll module delivery by 2-4 weeks, functional gaps requiring workarounds, and potential provider change costs. Risk of incomplete payroll workflows impacting end-user experience and business operations. May require additional development effort for custom UI/UX and API integration layers.
